description: Foundation world models like Genie 3, Genie 2, and V-JEPA 2 are emerging as core infrastructure for training AI systems to understand and predict physical dynamics. These models enable robots and autonomous systems to plan actions without real-world interaction, accelerating safe deployment.

Waymo's world model and related simulation advances are enabling more sophisticated testing and training of autonomous vehicles at scale. This represents a shift from real-world testing toward AI-driven simulation environments.

Projects like Gemini Robotics demonstrate how large multimodal models can be adapted to control physical systems and understand visual feedback. This bridges the gap between language/vision models and real-world robot manipulation.

Startups like Vassar Robotics are democratizing robotics with sub-$300 arms capable of learning new skills through imitation. This represents a major shift toward accessible robotic platforms for research and small-scale automation.

Ex-Waymo engineers launching Bedrock Robotics with $80M signals institutional confidence in robotics for construction automation. This vertical represents one of the first major physical AI deployment targets beyond autonomous vehicles.

The OpenUSD alliance (Pixar, Adobe, Apple, Autodesk, Nvidia) is establishing industry standards for 3D digital twins and simulation. This infrastructure layer is critical for scaling world models and robotics simulation across organizations.

Open-source tools like MuJoCo and emerging benchmarks (PhAIL) are enabling standardized evaluation of physical reasoning and robot learning. These tools are becoming essential infrastructure for the physical AI research community.

Increased venture funding and talent migration from established labs (Waymo, Meta, Google) into robotics startups signals maturation of the field. Educational interest (learning robotics in 2025) accompanies commercial deployment momentum.
